text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to get or send data. Many APIs return information in JSON format, which is easy for computers to read. First, you often need authentication, such as an API key, to access the service. Some APIs have rate limits, meaning you can only make a certain number of requests per hour. If you exceed this, the API might temporarily block you. It's also important to handle errors, like when a request fails or returns unexpected data. Good error handling helps your application run smoothly and avoid crashes, even if the API changes or has issues.
